freeze_boundary: This registry documents current and intended authority. It does not authorize new runtime behavior, schema migration, AI synthesis, or feature work.
rules: Suggestion producers may propose candidates but must not commit canonical truth directly.
Views may render and request explicit canonical actions but must not mutate by reading.
Operator shells may inspect and reason, but must not become silent runtime mutation authority.
Transport layers may deliver or acknowledge messages but must not decide behavioral meaning by themselves.

scan_contract.hard_fail_condition_later: S1c may promote selected missing-owner findings to CI failures after owners and exceptions are settled.

surfaces.owner: TaskManager plus canonical task/deadline binding endpoints
allowed_writes: create, update, soft-delete, reschedule, bind/unbind deadline, cache invalidation, explicit undo
surfaces.notes: Task route writes that bypass TaskManager are tolerated only as legacy compatibility until R4/R5 extraction.

allowed_writes: deadline create/update/void
terminal deadline reconciliation
provider completion candidate persistence
surfaces.notes: Moodle/iCal/WS provider facts must route here or remain candidates until S1b seals the path.

surfaces.owner: StopwatchManager and RedisClient stopwatch state methods
allowed_writes: start, pause, resume, switch, stop, stale resolution, active stopwatch Redis mutation
surfaces.notes: Only one active timer per user is allowed; planned tasks may overlap.

surfaces.owner: Output surface lifecycle APIs and exposure ledger
allowed_writes: decision, suppression, browser render, acknowledgement, dismissal/action outcome
surfaces.notes: Queue insertion and delivery are not exposure. Browser render creates render truth.

surfaces.owner: Notification lifecycle service and web notification host
allowed_writes: created, queued, reserved, delivered, rendered, acted, dismissed, expired, lost_unrendered
surfaces.notes: Notification lifecycle must stay separate from exposure render and interaction outcome truth.

surfaces.owner: Provider adapters plus canonical provider/deadline services
allowed_writes: provider sync status
provider facts/candidates
credential state through encrypted app path
surfaces.notes: Provider completion is evidence/candidate, not clean execution truth unless user-confirmed.

surfaces.owner: Users endpoints plus user_data_registry and Redis runtime purge
allowed_writes: consent, profile, export audit, delete request, hard/retained delete, Redis runtime purge, provider disconnect
surfaces.notes: Export/delete coverage must be registry-driven and secret-redacted.

surfaces.owner: IdentityResolver, UserProvisioningService, and RequestUserContext extraction targets
allowed_writes: auth audit/profile bootstrap only through current guarded request identity path
surfaces.notes: X-User-Id is test-only and must fail closed outside test/operator tooling. See docs/registries/identity_scoping_ownership.md.

allowed_writes: none
surfaces.notes: Model constructor names trip write-marker scans, but this file must not own runtime mutation authority.

allowed_writes: candidate/audit persistence only; canonical task/deadline writes route through TaskManager/DeadlineManager
surfaces.notes: LLM enrichment may annotate; deterministic deadline scoring owns candidate scoring during freeze.

surfaces.owner: Academic pressure map and output-surface lifecycle
allowed_writes: redacted pressure-map exposure render through output surface lifecycle only
surfaces.notes: Pressure Map may prioritize repair attention but must not own automatic mutation or student-risk scoring.

allowed_writes: operator alert queue append, confirmed delivery acknowledgement, dead-letter/retry state
surfaces.notes: S1b must ensure no destructive dequeue before confirmed send.

surfaces.owner: Cortex, registered computation modules, Admission/Coverage Gate, ClaimCompiler
allowed_writes: legacy exposure compatibility only until sealed
read-time metric computation
deterministic claim compilation
surfaces.notes: Analytics endpoints may expose compiled products, but do not own clean filters, confidence semantics, exposure writes, or claim authority.

surfaces.owner: Operator dashboard service and read-only admin compatibility
allowed_writes: none during dashboard reads
surfaces.notes: Operator/admin reads must not mutate last activity, notification state, exposure state, user metrics, task/session/provider state, or Redis runtime state.

surfaces.owner: Parked/read-only Jarvis compatibility until explicitly reauthorized
allowed_writes: audit rows only unless routed through canonical commands
surfaces.notes: Jarvis is not a second product mind. OpenClaw is the preferred future reasoning host, but no runtime AI synthesis is authorized.

surfaces.owner: Scheduler jobs routed through canonical services
allowed_writes: only through canonical service calls or explicitly documented worker-owned audit rows
surfaces.notes: Worker-owned direct writes are report-only findings until S1b/S1c decides enforcement.

allowed_writes: local/test/synthetic setup only
surfaces.notes: Synthetic/operator/test rows must be excluded or explicitly bucketed in readiness metrics.

parked_data_model_refactors.intent: Move provider-specific connection and encrypted credential state out of User.
blocked_by: no schema migration authority during freeze
provider credential read/write paths must first be sealed
